Text: Scientists were surprised to discover that the deep ocean trenches contain an {{c1::immense}} variety of life forms previously unknown to biology.
Why: 源自拉丁語 immensus（in- 不 + metiri 測量）— 字面義為「大到無法測量」。形容規模、程度或力量大到難以計量的境界。
Example: The athlete felt an immense sense of relief after crossing the finish line in first place.

Text: Her contributions during the research phase were {{c1::pivotal}} in determining the final direction of the architectural design project.
Why: 源自 pivot（樞軸）— 機器運轉時所有重量承載與旋轉的中心點，pivotal 即形容某個環節「像樞紐般」決定了全局的成敗。
Example: The invention of the printing press was a pivotal moment in the history of human communication.

Text: The morning fog lifted to reveal a scene of absolute {{c1::tranquility}} that seemed to stretch across the entire valley.
Why: 源自拉丁語 tranquillus（安寧），結合 trans-（穿過）與 quies（靜止）— 彷彿穿越重重喧囂後，抵達那種水波不興、心境澄澈的極致寧靜。
Example: Soft colors and minimalist furniture helped her maintain a sense of tranquility while working from home.

Text: The new project management software was designed to {{c1::facilitate}} communication between the different departments of the large international company.
Why: 源自拉丁文 facilis（容易）加上 -itate（使動詞化）— 字面意為「使事情變得容易」，即透過提供工具或環境來「促成、便利」某事發生。
Example: Hiring an experienced mediator helped facilitate a peaceful resolution to the long-standing family dispute.

Text: The soft lighting and acoustic jazz music created a cozy {{c1::ambiance}} that made everyone feel immediately relaxed and welcome.
Why: 源自法語 ambiant（周圍的），字首 ambi- 表示「在周圍」，就像圍繞在身邊的空氣與環境給人的整體感受。
Example: Designers often use specific color palettes to enhance the professional ambiance of modern office spaces and corporate lobbies.

Text: The school's new curriculum is designed to {{c1::foster}} a sense of community and collaboration among students from diverse backgrounds.
Why: 源自古英語 fostrian（養育、餵食），原指提供食物使之成長，現引申為透過營造環境來「培養」或「促進」某種抽象特質或發展。
Example: After years of living apart, the siblings worked hard to foster a closer relationship through regular phone calls.

Text: She was so {{c1::engrossed}} in her novel that she didn't hear the doorbell ring three times.
Why: 源自 en- (進入) + gross (大、全部) — 就像整個人「陷進去」某個巨大物體中，形容全神貫注到忘記周遭一切的入迷狀態。
Example: The scientist remained deeply engrossed in his research for hours, oblivious to the passing time.

Text: Teachers often notice that a typical {{c1::adolescent}} requires more sleep than younger children due to significant biological changes occurring during puberty.
Why: 源自拉丁語 adolescere（成長），指處於「成長中」的過渡期；-escent 字尾表示「正在變為...的過程」，貼切形容青少年的轉變。
Example: The clinic specializes in providing mental health support tailored specifically to the needs of the modern adolescent.

Text: Tourists from all over the world visit the capital to admire the unique {{c1::architectural}} features of the ancient cathedral.
Why: 源自 archi-（首要、大）與 tect（建造者）— 建築師是「首席建造者」，architectural 則形容這份融合結構與美學的設計美感。
Example: Modern city planning requires a deep understanding of both architectural design and sustainable environmental practices.

Text: After months of careful investigation, the detectives finally began to {{c1::unravel}} the complex web of lies surrounding the case.
Why: un-（除去）+ ravel（糾纏、編織物）— 想像線團被打結後一點點理清，引申為將「解開」混亂的局勢或「拆穿」複雜的計謀。
Example: The old sweater started to unravel at the seams after it was washed in the machine several times.

Text: After several warnings, the teacher gave the class a {{c1::stern}} look that immediately silenced everyone in the room.
Why: 形容詞，源自古英語 styrne（僵硬、嚴厲）。想像一個人的表情與原則像鐵石一樣僵硬、毫不退讓，這就是一種「嚴厲、不苟言笑」的威懾感。
Example: The mountain climbers faced stern conditions as the blizzard obscured their path to the summit.

Text: The detective found the anonymous letter quite {{c1::intriguing}} because it contained details that only the killer could have known.
Why: 源自拉丁字根 intricare（使糾結），形容某事像解不開的亂繩般引發強烈好奇心與探索慾，使其具有高度的吸引力。
Example: She presented an intriguing argument that challenged the long-held beliefs of the scientific community.

Text: Having a flexible schedule is a major {{c1::perk}} that allows parents to balance their professional and family responsibilities.
Why: 源自 perquisite (額外補貼) 的縮寫 — 原指職位附帶的特權。想像它是工資以外，能讓你工作時心情 'perk up' (振作、活潑) 的那份額外福利。
Example: Free coffee and snacks might seem like a small perk, but they can significantly improve office morale.

Text: After the heated argument, he {{c1::stomped}} out of the room and slammed the door loudly behind him to show his anger.
Why: 字音模擬重步砸地的聲響，帶有憤怒或沈重感的「跺腳、重步行走」，讓人聯想到腳掌與地面撞擊的強大重量。
Example: The angry toddler stomped his feet on the kitchen floor because he was denied a second cookie.

Text: The movie was {{c1::supposedly}} based on a true story, but many fans noticed several historical inaccuracies in the plot.
Why: 源自 suppose（假定、猜想）加上副詞字尾 — 用於轉述他人的說法，暗示「據說如此，但我對真實性語帶保留」，常用於描述傳聞與事實的落差。
Example: This new organic restaurant is supposedly the best in town, though the reviews are quite mixed.

Text: During the long hike, a swarm of aggressive mosquitoes continued to {{c1::harass}} the travelers until they finally reached the summit.
Why: 源自古法語 harer（唆使獵犬攻擊）。想像被獵犬盯上、反覆受其侵擾追趕的負擔感，引申為長期且蓄意的「騷擾」或「折磨」。
Example: The documentary showed how small predators often harass much larger animals to protect their territory from intrusion.
